summary: Necrotic myocardium does not metabolize tetrazolium chloride and thus shows little to no staining—resulting in a paler or blue hue compared to the pink/red-staining viable areas. At autopsy after cardiac death due to acute coronary syndrome, such findings indicate prior ischemia leading directly to cell death.
